channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Speaker Johnson makes clarification after statement regarding transgender House member
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., clarifies that "a man cannot become a woman" after a question about transgender Rep.-elect Sarah McBride.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans on Ethics Committee weigh 'unenviable' Gaetz report decision
item.description: Republicans on the House Ethics Committee are barreling into a no-win situation ahead of their expected meeting Wednesday to discuss their report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), weighing a high-stakes decision that will reverberate throughout Washington.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Top 10 states where Trump outperformed in 2024
item.description: President-elect Trump flipped six key swing states in his historic win this month, but his gains were nationwide, and some of his biggest were in areas that may surprise you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian national accused of funneling over $9 million in US-funded humanitarian aid to terrorist organization
item.description: The U.S. attorney's office said that a Syrian national was charged after allegedly funneling more than $9 million in U.S.-funded humanitarian aid to terrorist organizations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump gives one-word response when asked if he is reconsidering Matt Gaetz's AG nomination
item.description: President-elect Trump on Tuesday said he would not be reconsidering his nomination of former Congressman Matt Gaetz for attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A battle over mail ballots in Pennsylvania is latest example of messy disputes over election rules
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The recount underway in Pennsylvania's U.S. Senate race marks the end of a chaotic post-election period that has become the latest example of how disputed election rules can expose weak points in a core function of American democracy.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration to announce $275 million Ukraine weapons package this week
item.description: The Biden administration is expected to announce a $275 million presidential drawdown authority weapons package for Ukraine this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to nominate Mehmet Oz for CMS administrator
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he is nominating television personality Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) administrator. Oz unsuccessfully ran for Senate in Pennsylvania in 2022 with Trump's endorsement and has been a Trump ally ever since.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A GOP lawmaker introduces a Capitol bathroom rule before her trans colleague arrives
item.description: South Carolina Rep. Nancy Mace says the resolution aims to protect women's rights. Democratic Rep.-elect Sarah McBride, the first out trans person elected to Congress, has slammed it as a distraction.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore and Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Dr. Oz to run Medicare and Medicaid, Linda McMahon for Education, Lutnick for Commerce
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has tapped billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: RFK Jr.'s support of raw milk sheds light on debate
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's pick for Health and Human Services Secretary, Robert F Kennedy, Jr, has long been a proponent of natural food choices.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: The order is a win for McCormick and a loss for Casey as the campaigns prepare for a statewide recount and press counties for favorable ballot-counting decisions while election workers are sorting through thousands of provisional ballots.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Size of slim Republican House majority hangs on 5 uncalled races
item.description: There are still five uncalled House races that will determine the size of the Republican majority in Congress after the 2024 elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden and Xi take a first step to limit AI and nuclear decisions at their last meeting
item.description: The leaders agreed that humans should control decisions to use nuclear weapons — not AI. And they talked about the importance of stability during the transition to a new U.S. administration.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung to serve as White House communications director
item.description: Steven Cheung, who has served as a top press aide on the Trump campaign for the past two years, will join the incoming administration as White House communications director, President-elect Trump announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Florida GOP chair approves Gaetz AG nomination; state democratic chair concerned
item.description: There are increasing questions from political figures surrounding President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Justice Department. However, former Rep. Matt Gaetz is getting support from Evan Power, the chair of the Florida GOP.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: North Las Vegas city councilwoman wins re-election by 9 votes
item.description: North Las Vegas City Councilwoman Ruth Garcia-Anderson won re-election by 9 votes -- a difference of 0.04%, according to unofficial election results updated Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thousands of votes never counted across Nevada due to signature issues
item.description: More than 9,000 mail-in ballots across Nevada were not counted because voters did not verify their signatures before the curing deadline, according to data the 8 News Now Investigators reviewed.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Many Californians would back Kamala Harris for California governor, new poll suggests
item.description: It’s unclear what Vice President Kamala Harris will do after losing the presidential election, but should she run for California governor, support among state residents is seemingly strong, new polling suggests.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
